page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-12xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with a single thread per core configuration, operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is highly robust, featuring a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps, which optimizes the instance for high-throughput data transfers and Elastic Block Store operations.

Benchmark evaluations place the m8azn.metal-12xl in the top tier across multiple performance dimensions. It demonstrates exceptional computational capability with a stress-ng multi-core score of 201,425.18 and a PassMark CPU score of 116,193.1. Memory performance is equally strong, achieving a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 530.58 GB/sec. The system also excels in high-throughput operations, delivering 178.57 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 120.26 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture and high resource density of the m8azn.metal-12xl deliver significant value for workloads requiring dedicated hardware without virtualization overhead. The combination of a 5.0 GHz clock speed, 100 Gbps networking, and DDR5 memory makes this instance highly suitable for demanding applications such as high-performance web serving, database operations, memory-cached key-value stores, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-12xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server designed for high-performance workloads requiring dedicated hardware. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at 5.0 GHz, this instance features 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for storage and external communication. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for CPU and memory performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 116,193.1 and a Redis SET throughput of 178.57 million operations per second. The server offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for bare-metal deployments. It is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-12xl is a bare-metal server featuring a 48-core AMD EPYC processor at 5.0 GHz, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-12xl is a bare-metal server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server combining dedicated forty-eight core processing, rapid DDR5 memory, and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 48 physical cores running at 5.0 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and memory bandwidth
Optimized for bare-metal workloads, static web serving, and CPU-based inference
